candidates.hypothesis: A random stratified split reports materially higher macro-F1 than a whole-wilderness-area holdout under the same small MLP and budget after the four area-indicator columns are removed from model inputs.
candidates.mechanism: Random splitting mixes records from the same ecogeographic areas across train and test, while an area holdout creates a stronger distribution shift; UCI documents meaningful elevation and species differences among the four areas.
candidates.differentiating_claim: No method novelty is claimed; the useful output is a preregistered estimate of how strongly this evaluation choice changes the decision under one fixed protocol.
candidates.minimum_falsification_experiment: Freeze one random stratified split and one preselected area holdout, remove area indicators, run one seed for at most five epochs, and report macro-F1, per-class recall, class coverage, uncertainty, and measured GPU time without selecting the better-looking split after testing.
risks: Some held-out areas may omit target classes, so class coverage must be reported and unsupported comparisons must remain unsupported.
The dataset has no coordinates, so area holdout is not a complete spatial-generalization test.
Keeping the four area-indicator columns would leak the holdout identity into model inputs.
pivots: If preregistered area holdouts lack adequate class coverage, stop and request a new Gate 1 decision instead of choosing a favorable area.
If the pilot forecasts a budget overrun, keep only the pilot as an unsupported or qualified result.

candidates.hypothesis: The ten continuous cartographic features retain most of the all-feature macro-F1 while reducing reliance on wilderness-area and soil-type proxy variables.
candidates.mechanism: Elevation, slope, hydrology, road, fire-point, and hillshade features may contain much of the predictive structure, while the 44 binary group indicators encode location and soil categories.
candidates.differentiating_claim: No feature-selection novelty is claimed; the experiment would expose a decision-relevant performance-versus-proxy-use trade-off for this exact dataset and model.
candidates.minimum_falsification_experiment: Freeze one stratified split and one model configuration, then compare all 54 features against the ten continuous features for one seed with identical epochs and report macro-F1, worst-class recall, parameter count, and GPU time.
risks: The proxy interpretation is descriptive and cannot establish causal ecological relationships.
A random split can still overstate generalization beyond the four documented areas.
pivots: If all-feature performance dominates, report the trade-off rather than searching for a favorable subset.
If feature meanings differ from the UCI metadata after inspection, stop before training.

candidates.hypothesis: Training-set-derived inverse-frequency class weights improve worst-class recall without an unacceptable macro-F1 loss under a frozen stratified split.
candidates.mechanism: A weighted loss increases the contribution of less frequent classes during optimization.
candidates.differentiating_claim: This would be a transparent replication for workflow demonstration only; prior work already evaluates class-weighted and worst-class risks on Covertype.
candidates.minimum_falsification_experiment: After verifying class counts from the training split only, compare unweighted and inverse-frequency cross-entropy for one seed and reject the hypothesis if worst-class recall does not improve or macro-F1 crosses the preregistered harm threshold.
risks: Class weighting is established and cannot support a novelty claim.
Rare-class gains can trade off sharply against common-class performance.
pivots: If the inspected training split is not materially imbalanced, reject the candidate before training.
If weighted loss destabilizes training, retain the failure artifact and stop rather than tuning after test inspection.
